etymology.description: semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 睪(睪) (yì)
etymology.interpretation: In 擇(擇) (zé, "to pick"), the semantic component 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") conveys the action performed with a hand, which aligns with the meaning of choosing or picking. The phonetic component 睪(睪) (yì) contributes the sound aspect without adding to the meaning.
etymology.simplification: 择(擇) replaces 睪(睪) with a simplified form to reduce stroke count while keeping 手(手) for its semantic role.

examples.chinese: 我在市场上精心挑选水果(我在市場上精心挑選水果)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài shìchǎng shàng jīngxīn tiāoxuǎn shuǐguǒ.
examples.english: I carefully pick fruits at the market.

examples.chinese: 她会择一天去拜访老朋友(她會擇一天去拜訪老朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Tā huì zé yī tiān qù bàifǎng lǎo péngyǒu.
examples.english: She will choose a day to visit an old friend.

examples.chinese: 择善固执是他的人生信条(擇善固執是他的人生信條)。
examples.pinyin: Zé shàn gù zhí shì tā de rénshēng xìntiáo.
examples.english: To persist in choosing good is his life motto.
